reviews.review_msg: At first I did not get the feel for Adrienne's teaching methods, but as I took more of her classes she became a lot easier to understand. Definitely in my top five favorite teachers.
Avoid this professor if you want a boost in your GPA. Never had a harder marker.

EXTREMELY hard marker. Marked a 100 level course as if it were a 400 level course. Responds to emails well but wording is condescending. Course is nothing but writing. My advice is for you to AVOID this prof at all costs and take this class in a lecture, NOT online. Your GPA will thank you.
One of the better profs, although she was much harder than people say on here. Don't expect this to be a GPA booster whatsoever
Adrienne is a very sweet teacher who is excellent at lecturing. Sadly, the class was far more difficult than expected... just be prepared to put effort into it.
Sad that she's the best prof I've ever had in my first two years at SFU and she's only a sessional. Fair, helpful, clear, and an overall great prof. I would happily take another course with her
Had her for both CRIM 101 and 104 and she was very fair. She gives excellent notes and is more than helpful. Would definitely take a class with her again
Very helpful prof! Gives you plenty of time to copy down her very comprehensive notes, and marking is very fair.
I had her for CRIM 101 too and she was great in both courses. Ironic how she is only a sessional yet she is easily one of the bests profs in the CRIM department. Very approachable and fair

Easily my favourite prof I've had so far. Gives you time to write down notes fully, decently easy content, tells you the written question for the final ahead of time. Also when I couldn't get into CRIM classes this semester she talked to the department about opening classes for us. Super nice and glad I have her for 104 this semester again.
